completion_promises.criterion_text: src/fleet_memory/mcp/tools/supersede.py exists and registers memory_supersede via the TASK-MCP-001 extension point
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/fleet_memory/mcp/tools/supersede.py with memory_supersede implementation and register() function. Updated src/fleet_memory/mcp/server.py register_all() to call supersede.register(mcp, context).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Declaring that a newer memory supersedes an older one marks the older as superseded_by the newer; the older no longer appears in default search results
completion_promises.evidence: The memory_supersede tool calls apply_supersessions which marks predecessors with superseded_by field. Default search exclusion is handled by the retrieval contract (RD-6) as documented in the task.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A single-predecessor declaration is accepted and marks that one predecessor
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented and verified in test_single_predecessor_accepted. Tool accepts a single predecessor in the list and calls apply_supersessions.

completion_promises.criterion_text: An empty predecessor list is rejected with 'at least one predecessor is required' (ASSUM-005) — not a silent no-op
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented validation that raises ValueError with message 'At least one predecessor is required' when predecessor_keys is empty. Verified in test_empty_predecessor_list_rejected.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A malformed predecessor reference (e.g. not-a-key) is rejected with 'not a valid memory key' and no supersession is applied
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented _validate_natural_key() helper and validation logic that checks type:project:identifier format. Raises ValueError with 'not a valid memory key' message for malformed keys. Verified in test_malformed_predecessor_rejected and test_malformed_successor_rejected.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A memory superseding itself is rejected with 'a memory cannot supersede itself' and no supersession is applied
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented validation that checks if successor_key is in predecessor_keys and raises ValueError with message 'A memory cannot supersede itself'. Verified in test_self_supersession_rejected and test_self_supersession_in_list_rejected.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Forward supersession: declaring supersession of a predecessor that has not been written is accepted and takes effect once it is written
completion_promises.evidence: The tool calls apply_supersessions which already handles forward supersession (as documented in writer/supersession.py). When a predecessor doesn't exist, apply_supersessions accepts the declaration and the link takes effect when the predecessor is written. Verified in test_forward_supersession_accepted.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the store raises TimeoutError the tool returns 'memory store unavailable' and the server stays running (via TASK-MCP-002)
completion_promises.evidence: Wrapped memory_supersede with @tool_safe decorator from TASK-MCP-002, which catches TimeoutError and converts to ToolResult with infrastructure error type and 'memory store unavailable' message. Verified in test_store_timeout_degrades_gracefully.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'python -m ruff check' and 'python -m black --check' on all implementation and test files. All checks passed with zero errors.
